channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ruto Responds to Tinubu's Comments on Economic Conditions and English Usage
item.description: Kenyan President William Ruto has criticised remarks made by Nigerian President Bola Tinubu about Nigeria's economic situation and English proficiency. The exchange started when Tinubu, during a visit...

item.title: Canada's Economy and Ecosystem: The Case for Recognizing Natural Assets
item.description: A new report from Canada’s Nature Advantage coalition emphasizes the importance of the country's natural assets—such as clean air, water, and biodiversity—in supporting economic activity. The group ar...

item.title: Inquiry Report Details Causes and Impact of Tanzania’s 2025 Post-Election Violence
item.description: A Presidential Commission of Inquiry in Tanzania has released a detailed report explaining the scale, causes, and effects of the unrest that followed the October 2025 General Election. The commission,...

item.title: Mali's Education Crisis: Jihadist Influence and Community Resilience
item.description: The ongoing crisis in Mali is severely impacting education, with jihadist groups actively targeting schools and disrupting learning. This has resulted in widespread desertion, particularly in rural ar...

item.title: Fitch Revises Philippines Outlook to Negative Amid Economic and Governance Concerns
item.description: On April 20, 2026, Fitch Ratings changed the Philippines’ outlook from “stable” to “negative,” while keeping the long-term credit rating at BBB. Although technically not a downgrade, this negative out...

item.title: US sailor injured by monkey during stopover en route to Strait of Hormuz minesweeping mission
item.description: A US Navy sailor, scheduled to participate in mine clearance operations in the Strait of Hormuz, was injured in an unusual incident while on a stopover in Phuket, Thailand. The sailor was scratched by...

item.title: NBI files human trafficking complaints against relatives of Mike Defensor
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has filed human trafficking complaints against the wife and son of former Philippine official Mike Defensor, according to a report by ABS-CBN News. While the...

item.title: 7 Nigerian Food Pairings That Might Be Bad for Your Health
item.description: Food pairings are an integral part of Nigerian cuisine, often seen as the perfect combinations for a fulfilling meal. However, not all food pairings are as good for our bodies as they are for our tast...

item.title: Government Strengthens Industry Partnerships to Improve Vocational Skills Training
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has reaffirmed its commitment to improving skills-based education by strengthening cooperation with industries so that graduates gain practical and job-relevant competencies...

item.title: Eli Lilly declines government subsidy for Mounjaro, leaving many Australians without affordable access
item.description: An estimated 450,000 Australians may miss out on affordable access to the weight loss medication Mounjaro, after pharmaceutical company Eli Lilly rejected a government-subsidised pricing offer. The Ph...

item.title: Iran-Pakistan talks emerge as fragile ceasefires and global economic tensions persist
item.description: Iran is sending a delegation to Pakistan for diplomatic talks, according to regional sources, as efforts continue to manage tensions linked to the ongoing conflict involving the United States, Israel,...

item.title: APGA fixes April 30 deadline for member revalidation and registration ahead of 2027 elections
item.description: The All Progressives Grand Alliance (APGA) don announce say April 30, 2026 go be the final deadline for both new member registration and old member revalidation across Nigeria, as dem dey prepare for ...

item.title: Paris bids for ice hockey events in 2030 Winter Games after Nice mayor's opposition
item.description: Paris has put forward a bid to host ice hockey events during the 2030 Winter Games, after opposition from Nice's mayor, Eric Ciotti, who disagrees with converting the Allianz Arena into a temporary ic...

item.title: Long Waitlists and Exclusivity Define Access to the Raya Dating App
item.description: The article explores the growing frustration among people ովքեր have spent years waiting to be accepted into Raya, an exclusive, members-only dating app known for its celebrity users and curated commu...

item.title: US soldier allegedly earned $400K by betting on failed Maduro raid
item.description: A U.S. soldier is under investigation after reportedly earning $400,000 by betting on the failed attempt to capture Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro. The soldier allegedly used a popular online bet...

item.title: Claude Expands App Integrations to Personal Services Like Spotify, Uber, and More
item.description: Anthropic's AI model, Claude, is now expanding its app integrations beyond workplace tools to personal services. Users can now connect apps like Spotify, Uber, AllTrails, TripAdvisor, Instacart, and T...

item.title: Pakistan to Host Second Round of US-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: A high-level Iranian delegation, headed by Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, is expected to land in Islamabad later tonight for what seems like a very important round of peace talks with the United Sta...

item.title: Selections from TS Eliot’s Works: Poetry, Plays, and Critical Essays
item.description: The Scroll article presents an excerpt from newly released volumes that compile selections of T.S. Eliot’s poetry, plays, and critical essays. The pieces showcased include his evocative poem 'To the I...

item.title: Alberta to adopt year-round daylight saving time, prompting safety and tourism discussions
item.description: The Alberta government has announced that the province will move to permanent daylight saving time, a decision that has sparked debate among residents, experts, and industry stakeholders. Advocates of...

item.title: Arson Attempts on Melbourne Venues Continue, Motive Remains Unclear
item.description: A string of arson attacks targeting licensed venues in Melbourne continues, with recent attempts including a foiled one at the French restaurant France-Soir in South Yarra. Police have made several ar...
